memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: The American Diplomat
memes.description: An unnamed American diplomat involved in the international coordination following the kidnapping of Thomas Roch.

memes.label: The American Naval Commander
memes.description: The unnamed American naval officer who commanded the US squadron searching for Ker Karraje in the waters around Bermuda after Thomas Roch's kidnapping.

memes.label: The American Secretary of the Navy
memes.description: The American cabinet official who ordered the naval search for Thomas Roch after his kidnapping from Healthful House.

memes.label: Atlantic Ocean
memes.description: The Atlantic Ocean serves as the principal maritime theater in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag, encompassing the routes of the pirate schooner Ebba and the hidden location of Back Cup.

memes.label: Back Cup
memes.description: Isolated islet in the Bermuda archipelago serving as the pirates' hidden base

memes.label: Back Cup Cavern
memes.description: The vast subterranean cavern within Back Cup that serves as Ker Karraje's hidden pirate base in Facing the Flag

memes.label: The Back Cup Explosion
memes.description: The Back Cup Explosion is the catastrophic climax of Facing the Flag, in which the pirate stronghold is destroyed by the very weapon Ker Karraje sought to control — Thomas Roch's Fulgurator.

memes.label: The Exterior of Back Cup
memes.description: The barren, unbroken exterior of Back Cup that concealed the elaborate pirate base within, making the islet appear uninhabited to passing vessels.

memes.label: The Back Cup Lagoon
memes.description: The internal body of water within Back Cup's cavern, accessible only through the submarine tunnel, where the pirate vessels were moored.

memes.label: The Back Cup Sentry
memes.description: A pirate stationed as a guard within the Back Cup cavern, monitoring the prisoners and maintaining internal security.

memes.label: Battle Between the Sword and the Tug
memes.description: The naval engagement between the pirate submarine and a pursuing vessel

memes.label: Bermuda
memes.description: The Bermuda archipelago is a British territory in the North Atlantic, serving as a geographical reference point for the pirate operations based at Back Cup in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag.

memes.label: The Bermuda Archipelago
memes.description: The island group in the North Atlantic where Back Cup was located and around which the international naval coalition concentrated its search.

memes.label: British Admiralty
memes.description: The British naval authority that negotiated with Thomas Roch for his invention

memes.label: The British Admiralty Official
memes.description: An unnamed senior official of the British Admiralty who authorized the search for Ker Karraje and committed British naval forces to the international coalition.

memes.label: The British Negotiator
memes.description: The unnamed British official who attempted to extract Thomas Roch's fulgurator secret through negotiation.

memes.label: Cape Hatteras
memes.description: The prominent cape on the North Carolina coast, a navigational landmark for vessels entering and leaving Pamlico Sound and the Atlantic approaches.

memes.label: Captain Chapel
memes.description: Artillery captain mentioned in reference to engines in Facing the Flag

memes.label: Captain Spade
memes.description: Captain Spade is the commander of the pirate schooner Ebba and a key subordinate of Count d'Artigas (Ker Karraje), responsible for maritime operations and the execution of Thomas Roch's kidnapping in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag.

memes.label: Character Index
memes.description: Complete index of characters appearing in Jules Verne's "Facing the Flag" (1896)

memes.label: Charleston
memes.description: South Carolina port city where the fulgurator was publicly tested and where Ker Karraje's schooner Ebba was sighted

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Index of key concepts, technologies, and themes in "Facing the Flag"

memes.label: Count d'Artigas
memes.description: Alternative spelling of Count d'Artigas; the alias of pirate chief Ker Karraje

memes.label: Count d'Artigas
memes.description: Count d'Artigas, revealed to be the notorious pirate Ker Karraje, is the primary antagonist of Jules Verne's Facing the Flag — a sophisticated criminal who orchestrates the kidnapping of Thomas Roch to acquire the devastating Fulgurator weapon.

memes.label: Count d'Artigas (Alias)
memes.description: The aristocratic alias adopted by Ker Karraje to infiltrate Healthful House and mask his identity as a pirate leader.

memes.label: The Deflagrator Negotiation
memes.description: The tense negotiations at Back Cup over Thomas Roch's deflagrator, the second secret without which the fulgurator explosive was valueless, representing the climax of the pirates' attempt to extract Roch's full knowledge.

memes.label: Director of Healthful House
memes.description: The director of Healthful House, the private sanatorium in North Carolina where Thomas Roch is held at the beginning of Jules Verne's Facing the Flag.

memes.label: Disguise and Identity
memes.description: The thematic exploration of disguise, assumed identities, and hidden selves in Facing the Flag, where nearly every major character operates under a false or concealed identity.

memes.label: The Ebba Deckhand
memes.description: An unnamed deckhand aboard the Ebba schooner who participated in the kidnapping voyage to Back Cup.

memes.label: The Helmsman of the Ebba
memes.description: The unnamed sailor who steered the schooner Ebba during the kidnapping voyage from Pamlico Sound to Back Cup under Captain Spade's command.

memes.label: The Night Departure of the Ebba
memes.description: The nocturnal departure of the Ebba from New Berne with the kidnapped Thomas Roch and Simon Hart aboard, beginning the voyage to Back Cup.

memes.label: Ebba Schooner
memes.description: The Ebba is the pirate schooner commanded by Captain Spade and owned by Count d'Artigas (Ker Karraje), serving as the mobile operational platform for the Back Cup pirate organization in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag.

memes.label: Ebba Voyage to Back Cup
memes.description: The voyage of the schooner Ebba transporting the kidnapped Thomas Roch and Simon Hart from Healthful House to the pirate stronghold at Back Cup.

memes.label: Effrondat
memes.description: Effrondat is the boatswain of the pirate schooner Ebba, responsible for deck operations under Captain Spade's command in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag.

memes.label: Engineer Serko
memes.description: Engineer Serko is a key subordinate of Count d'Artigas and Ker Karraje, responsible for managing the technical operations at the Back Cup pirate stronghold in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag.

memes.label: England
memes.description: The colonial power that annexed Bermuda, home of Lieutenant Davon, and a key nation in the international response to Ker Karraje's piracy

memes.label: Escape from Back Cup
memes.description: The Escape from Back Cup is the climactic event of Jules Verne's Facing the Flag, in which Simon Hart and Thomas Roch are rescued from the pirate stronghold as the cavern faces destruction.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Chronological index of major events in "Facing the Flag"

memes.label: Explosive Technology in Facing the Flag
memes.description: The treatment of explosive and projectile technology in the novel, reflecting late 19th-century anxieties about weapons of mass destruction and the arms race.

memes.label: Face au drapeau
memes.description: The original 1896 French edition of Jules Verne's novel

memes.label: Facing the Flag (novel)
memes.description: Jules Verne's 1896 novel about a mad inventor and his devastating weapon

memes.label: Falcon (US Cruiser)
memes.description: United States cruiser stationed in Bermuda waters, tasked with intercepting the schooner Ebba during the search for the abducted inventor Thomas Roch.

memes.label: The Final Naval Battle at Back Cup
memes.description: The climactic naval engagement between the international coalition and Ker Karraje's forces at Back Cup, culminating in Thomas Roch's patriotic recognition and the destruction of the pirate base.

memes.label: France
memes.description: The home country of Simon Hart and Thomas Roch, and the first nation to which the fulgurator was offered

memes.label: The French Commission Member
memes.description: An unnamed member of the French government commission that evaluated and rejected Thomas Roch's fulgurator invention.

memes.label: The Raising of the French Flag
memes.description: The climactic moment when the French warship raised the tricolor approaching Back Cup, the visual signal that triggered Thomas Roch's patriotic recognition and the novel's resolution.

memes.label: French Government Commission
memes.description: The French government body that first rejected Thomas Roch's fulgurator

memes.label: The French Government Minister
memes.description: The French Government Minister is the official who, alongside Effrondat, represents the French state's refusal to purchase Thomas Roch's Fulgurator — a bureaucratic decision with catastrophic consequences in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag.

memes.label: The French Naval Captain
memes.description: The unnamed commander of the French warship whose approach to Back Cup, flying the tricolor, triggered Thomas Roch's patriotic recognition and the novel's climax.

memes.label: Fulgurator
memes.description: Thomas Roch's devastating military invention, the central MacGuffin of the novel

memes.label: Fulgurator Demonstration
memes.description: The public and private demonstrations of Thomas Roch's Fulgurator weapon, showcasing its devastating destructive power.

memes.label: The Fulgurator Engine
memes.description: The Fulgurator Engine is Thomas Roch's revolutionary military invention — a weapon of unprecedented destructive power — that drives the central conflict of Jules Verne's Facing the Flag as multiple parties compete to possess its secret.

memes.label: Gaydon
memes.description: Alias of Simon Hart, the warder of Thomas Roch at Healthful House

memes.label: Genius and Madness
memes.description: The thin boundary between creative genius and mental instability as embodied by Thomas Roch in Facing the Flag

memes.label: The German Representative
memes.description: An unnamed German official among the foreign powers that Thomas Roch approached with his fulgurator invention after being rejected by France.

memes.label: Germany
memes.description: One of the European great powers that Thomas Roch approached with his fulgurator invention after being rejected by France.

memes.label: The Gulf of Mexico
memes.description: The body of water south of the United States, referenced in connection with the naval search for Ker Karraje after Roch's kidnapping.

memes.label: Simon Hart's Final Report
memes.description: The implied framing of the novel as Simon Hart's official report to the French government after his rescue, explaining the events at Back Cup.

memes.label: Simon Hart's Recognition of the French Flag
memes.description: The moment when Simon Hart, imprisoned at Back Cup, recognizes the approaching French warship by its tricolor flag, realizing that rescue is at hand.

memes.label: Healthful House
memes.description: Private sanatorium in North Carolina where Thomas Roch is confined

memes.label: The Grounds of Healthful House
memes.description: The extensive parkland surrounding Healthful House, providing both therapeutic environment for patients and the perimeter breached during the kidnapping of Thomas Roch.

memes.label: The Healthful House Groundskeeper
memes.description: A minor staff member at Healthful House whose observations of the grounds on the night of the kidnapping contributed to the understanding of how the crime was executed.

memes.label: The Healthful House Pharmacist
memes.description: A staff member at Healthful House who prepared Thomas Roch's medications and observed the unusual interest shown by Count d'Artigas in the facility's most famous patient.

memes.label: Healthful House Surveillance
memes.description: The system of surveillance and observation maintained over Thomas Roch at Healthful House, foreshadowing the kidnapping plot.

memes.label: The Visit of Count d'Artigas to Healthful House
memes.description: Ker Karraje's reconnaissance visit to Healthful House, disguised as Count d'Artigas, during which he assessed the security of the facility in preparation for the kidnapping of Thomas Roch.

memes.label: Honor and Duty
memes.description: The thematic exploration of honor, duty, and moral obligation in Facing the Flag, represented primarily through Simon Hart's sense of patriotic duty and professional responsibility.

memes.label: Imprisonment and Captivity
memes.description: The experience of captivity as a central narrative condition in Facing the Flag, affecting both Thomas Roch and Simon Hart

memes.label: The Formation of the International Coalition
memes.description: The diplomatic and military process by which the naval forces of France, Britain, the United States, and other powers coordinated their response to the threat posed by Ker Karraje.

memes.label: International Maritime Law
memes.description: The legal framework governing maritime conduct in the novel, which Ker Karraje exploits and the international coalition ultimately enforces.

memes.label: The Inventor as Tragic Hero
memes.description: The literary archetype of the brilliant inventor whose gifts are undermined by personal flaws, with Thomas Roch as a prime example in Verne's late fiction.

memes.label: The Italian Representative
memes.description: An unnamed Italian official among the international representatives who approached Thomas Roch seeking to acquire the fulgurator for Italy.

memes.label: Jules Verne
memes.description: French novelist (1828–1905), author of "Face au drapeau" (1896), the source novel for this wiki.

memes.label: Ker Karraje
memes.description: Malay pirate chief, the principal antagonist, also known as Count d'Artigas

memes.label: The Discovery of Ker Karraje's Identity
memes.description: The gradual process by which Simon Hart deduced that Count d'Artigas was actually the notorious pirate Ker Karraje, confirming his worst suspicions.

memes.label: Kidnapping of Thomas Roch
memes.description: The nighttime abduction of Thomas Roch and Simon Hart from Healthful House

memes.label: Lieutenant Davon
memes.description: Lieutenant Davon is a British naval officer who appears at the climax of Jules Verne's Facing the Flag, representing the international naval response to the pirate threat posed by Ker Karraje's organization at Back Cup.

memes.label: Location Index
memes.description: Index of locations and geographical settings in "Facing the Flag"

memes.label: Loyalty Testing
memes.description: The repeated tests of loyalty that Simon Hart faces at Back Cup, where Engineer Serko and Ker Karraje probe whether "Gaydon" can be turned or trusted.

memes.label: Madness and Sanity
memes.description: The thematic exploration of mental instability in Facing the Flag, where Thomas Roch's alternating madness and lucidity drives both the plot and the novel's philosophical concerns.

memes.label: Maritime Isolation
memes.description: The theme of physical and psychological isolation created by the maritime setting, where Back Cup's oceanic remoteness enables both concealment and psychological pressure.

memes.label: The Mediterranean Sea
memes.description: The sea where Ker Karraje operated as a pirate before relocating to the Atlantic and establishing his base at Back Cup.

memes.label: Mental Alienation
memes.description: The psychological condition of Thomas Roch, a central theme in the novel

memes.label: Naval Blockade and Interdiction
memes.description: The naval strategy employed by the international coalition to contain Ker Karraje at Back Cup, representing the institutional response to maritime lawlessness.

memes.label: Neuse River
memes.description: The river in North Carolina on whose estuary Healthful House is situated, and the waterway through which the kidnapping of Thomas Roch is executed

memes.label: The Mouth of the Neuse River
memes.description: The estuarine area where the Neuse River meets Pamlico Sound, serving as the anchorage for the Ebba before its kidnapping voyage.

memes.label: New Berne, North Carolina
memes.description: The town in North Carolina near Healthful House, from which the Ebba departed on its kidnapping voyage.

memes.label: The New Berne Wharf
memes.description: The wharf at New Berne, North Carolina, from which visitors to Healthful House disembarked and near which the Ebba was moored before its departure.

memes.label: Nineteenth-Century Psychiatry in Facing the Flag
memes.description: The historical context of psychiatric understanding in the 1890s that informs the novel's depiction of Thomas Roch's mental condition and his treatment at Healthful House.

memes.label: North Carolina
memes.description: U.S. state where Healthful House is located, serving as the novel's initial setting and the site of the kidnapping that launches the plot

memes.label: Organization Index
memes.description: Index of organizations, vessels, and groups in "Facing the Flag"

memes.label: The Outer Banks
memes.description: The chain of barrier islands off the North Carolina coast that separates Pamlico Sound from the Atlantic Ocean.

memes.label: Pacific Ocean
memes.description: The ocean where Ker Karraje began his career of piracy before relocating to the Atlantic and Back Cup

memes.label: Pamlico Sound
memes.description: Body of water off the North Carolina coast near Healthful House

memes.label: The Pamlico Sound Lighthouse
memes.description: The navigational landmark at the entrance to Pamlico Sound, noted by the crew of the Ebba during their night departure.

memes.label: The Waters of Pamlico Sound
memes.description: The navigable channels of Pamlico Sound through which the Ebba sailed during its kidnapping voyage to the Atlantic.

memes.label: Paris, France
memes.description: The capital of France, seat of the French government that first rejected Thomas Roch's fulgurator, and the symbolic homeland to which Simon Hart's patriotism is directed.

memes.label: Patriotism
memes.description: The theme of national loyalty that defines the novel's moral framework

memes.label: The Pirate Boatswain
memes.description: The unnamed boatswain of the pirate crew at Back Cup, responsible for the maintenance and operation of the vessels moored in the lagoon.

memes.label: Pirate Crew of Back Cup
memes.description: The Pirate Crew of Back Cup is the criminal organization led by Ker Karraje, operating from a hidden cavern in the Atlantic Ocean, whose ambition to weaponize Thomas Roch's Fulgurator drives the central conflict of Jules Verne's Facing the Flag.

memes.label: Pirate Crew of Back Cup
memes.description: The pirate crew operating from Back Cup under Ker Karraje's command

memes.label: The Pirate Lookout
memes.description: The pirate stationed as lookout at Back Cup, responsible for monitoring maritime approaches and alerting Ker Karraje to approaching vessels.

memes.label: Pirate Warfare
memes.description: Pirate warfare in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag represents a modernized form of maritime crime, combining traditional piracy with advanced technology and organizational sophistication under the leadership of Ker Karraje.

memes.label: Rejection and Resentment
memes.description: The psychological theme of institutional rejection and its corrosive effect on Thomas Roch, whose resentment toward the governments that spurned him fueled his descent.

memes.label: Roch's Fulgurator
memes.description: Thomas Roch's revolutionary military invention as a work of engineering genius

memes.label: Rejection of Roch's Invention
memes.description: The series of rejections by multiple governments that drove Roch to despair

memes.label: Thomas Roch's Intervals of Lucidity
memes.description: The alternating periods of sanity and madness experienced by Thomas Roch, during which his true character and patriotic instincts briefly surfaced.

memes.label: Roch's Offer to Britain
memes.description: Thomas Roch's approach to the British government with his fulgurator invention, part of the series of international offers that followed his rejection by France.

memes.label: Roch's Offer to Germany
memes.description: Thomas Roch's approach to the German government with his fulgurator invention after being rejected by France, one of the series of international offers that preceded his confinement at Healthful House.

memes.label: The Russian Representative
memes.description: An unnamed Russian official among the foreign powers approached by Thomas Roch with his fulgurator invention.

memes.label: The Sargasso Sea
memes.description: The region of the North Atlantic near Bermuda, referenced in connection with the maritime environment surrounding Back Cup.

memes.label: Scientific Secrecy
memes.description: The ethical tension between scientific openness and military secrecy surrounding Thomas Roch's fulgurator in Facing the Flag

memes.label: Simon Hart
memes.description: French engineer who disguises himself as Gaydon, keeper of Thomas Roch

memes.label: Gaydon (Simon Hart's Alias)
memes.description: The alias adopted by Simon Hart to work as Thomas Roch's keeper at Healthful House, part of the novel's pervasive pattern of disguised identities.

memes.label: Simon Hart's Captivity at Back Cup
memes.description: The period of Simon Hart's imprisonment at Back Cup, during which he maintained his alias as Gaydon while covertly gathering intelligence about Ker Karraje's operation.

memes.label: St. George, Bermuda
memes.description: One of the principal ports of Bermuda, serving as a key setting in the novel's finale when news of Thomas Roch's fate reaches the outside world.

memes.label: Submarine Design in Facing the Flag
memes.description: Submarine Design in Facing the Flag examines Jules Verne's vision of underwater vessels — the submarine tug, the submerged access to Back Cup, and the broader naval engineering context that places this novel within Verne's maritime technological imagination.

memes.label: Submarine Technology
memes.description: Submarine technology is a central motif in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag, representing both the pinnacle of maritime engineering and the means by which the pirates of Back Cup achieve their clandestine mobility.

memes.label: Submarine Tunnel
memes.description: The submerged passage providing the only maritime access to Back Cup's interior cavern in Facing the Flag

memes.label: Submarine Tunnel Engineering
memes.description: The engineering concept behind the submarine tunnel at Back Cup, representing the intersection of natural geological formation and human technical adaptation.

memes.label: Surveillance and Observation
memes.description: The pervasive theme of watching and being watched in Facing the Flag, from Roch's confinement to Hart's covert observation of his captors

memes.label: Technology and Ethics
memes.description: The ethical dimension of technological innovation in Facing the Flag, where the fulgurator raises questions about the moral responsibility of inventors and the proper limits of scientific inquiry.

memes.label: Thomas Roch
memes.description: French inventor of the fulgurator, confined at Healthful House

memes.label: Tonnant (French Cruiser)
memes.description: French cruiser that arrives at Back Cup during the climactic battle, inadvertently triggering Thomas Roch's patriotic awakening at the sight of the tricolor flag.

relations.label: Battle Between the Sword and the Tug

memes.label: The Tricolor Flag
memes.description: The French national flag that serves as the central symbol of patriotism in the novel's climax, when Thomas Roch's recognition of it triggers his ultimate act of loyalty.

memes.label: The United States of America
memes.description: The nation that assumed custody of Thomas Roch and the setting for the novel's opening chapters at Healthful House in North Carolina.

memes.label: United States Navy
memes.description: The American naval force that becomes involved in the pursuit of Ker Karraje

memes.label: Verne's Late Period (1890s)
memes.description: The final phase of Jules Verne's career (1890s–1905), characterized by darker themes, technological pessimism, and more complex moral frameworks than his earlier optimistic adventures.

memes.label: The Warder of Healthful House
memes.description: The Warder is the guard assigned to watch over Thomas Roch at Healthful House in Jules Verne's Facing the Flag, representing the institutional surveillance that both protects and confines the unstable inventor.

memes.label: Washington, D.C.
memes.description: The capital of the United States, seat of the American government that assumed custody of Thomas Roch after France's rejection.

memes.label: Wealth and Greed
memes.description: The thematic exploration of wealth as motivation in Facing the Flag, where Thomas Roch's demand for payment and Ker Karraje's lust for plunder drive the central conflict.
